heading: Established Program to Stimulate Competitive Research
label: 1862g Established Program to Stimulate Competitive Research
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Science Foundation Authorization Act of 1988, and not as part of the National Science Foundation Act of 1950 which comprises this chapter.

Amendments2017—, substituted “Established” for “Experimental” in section catchline.Pub. L. 114–329, § 103(e)(2)(A)Subsec. (a). , substituted “a program to stimulate competitive research (known as the ‘Established Program to Stimulate Competitive Research’)” for “an Experimental Program to Stimulate Competitive Research” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 114–329, § 103(e)(2)(B)Subsec. (b). , substituted “the Program” for “the program”.Pub. L. 114–329, § 103(e)(2)(C)

Planning Grants, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–368, § 26116 Stat. 3067Dec. 19, 2002“The Director is authorized to accept planning proposals from applicants who are within .075 percentage points of the current eligibility level for the Experimental Program to Stimulate Competitive Research. Such proposals shall be reviewed by the Foundation to determine their merit for support under the Experimental Program to Stimulate Competitive Research or any other appropriate program.”[For definitions of terms used in , set out above, see , set out as a note under .]section 26 of Pub. children.text: historically have received relatively little Federal research and development funding; and
have demonstrated a commitment to develop their research bases and improve science and engineering research and education programs at their universities and colleges.

children.label: b
children.text: A State which has received an initial award under such Program, whether or not the award was received before or after , shall be eligible for up to 5 years of additional support under the Program if that State provides assurances of new matching funds and submits an acceptable new plan for using Program funds and matching funds to build the research capabilities of the State.October 31, 1988
